facts.text: Founded in 1875, Oviedo evolved from a 40‑family settlement near Lake Jesup into a thriving trading post within a few years.
Lake Gem, the city’s centerpiece, was named by the Boston family after a close relative, reflecting early settlers’ personal ties.
In 1875, African‑American pioneer Mathew Powell founded the Antioch Missionary Baptist Church, one of Oviedo’s oldest standing institutions.
Established in 1978, the adjacent Central Florida Research Park has grown into Florida’s largest research park, driving Oviedo’s modern economy.
During 2008, Tropical Storm Fay hovered over Oviedo for days, delivering heavy rains and flooding that tested the city’s infrastructure.
Oviedo’s downtown historic district showcases buildings largely constructed between the late 19th and early 20th centuries, preserving its original charm.
